conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information from you. Let me start by asking:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children I'd like to ask about - Alex who's 3, Blake who's 8, and Charlie who's 2.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children: Alex (3 years old), Blake (8 years old), and Charlie (2 years old).

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of them, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start with:

**Are you responsible for all three children - meaning do they live with you and you're responsible for raising them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, all three of them live with me and I'm responsible for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. Now I need to understand your residency situation:

**Do you normally live in the UK, and are you or the children subject to immigration control (meaning do you have any restrictions on your right to reside in the UK)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. And no, there are no immigration restrictions - we have full right to be here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask about each child individually to ensure I assess their eligibility correctly.

**Are any of your three children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) currently in hospital, in care, or living with someone else for any period of time?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in hospital or in care, and they all live with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else also claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question:

**Are all three children under 16 years old, or if any are 16 or over, are they in approved education or training?**

(I can see Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2, so they're all under 16, but I need to confirm this for completeness.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - they're all under 16. Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Before I make my final assessment, let me review the official Child Benefit eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, I can now provide you with my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children.**

**Summary:**
- ✅ **Alex (age 3)** - Eligible
- ✅ **Blake (age 8)** - Eligible  
- ✅ **Charlie (age 2)** - Eligible

You meet all the requirements for Child Benefit:
- All three children are under 16 years old
- They all live with you and you're responsible for them
- You live in the UK with no immigration restrictions
- None of the children are in hospital or care
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you may wish to use the Child Benefit tax calculator to see if this applies to you.

You can proceed with applying for Child Benefit for all three children. Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit eligibility?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with you full-time, you are responsible for raising them, you live in the UK with no immigration restrictions, they are not in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with you full-time, you are responsible for raising them, you live in the UK with no immigration restrictions, they are not in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Blake meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Charlie is 2 years old (under 16), lives with you full-time, you are responsible for raising them, you live in the UK with no immigration restrictions, they are not in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Charlie meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie). You meet all the key requirements: you are responsible for children under 16, they live with you in the UK, you have no immigration restrictions, none of the children are in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. You can claim Child Benefit for all three children.
